item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Containment procedures are not very good, but it is an interesting anomaly with nice tests.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…D-1415-02 also developed female reproductive organs, becoming a hermaphrodite…

That should specify whether it is a fully functioning hermaphrodite or one where only one of said sex organs work, since humans are naturally not capable of having both working sex organs.
Other than that I feel like this is a rather good SCP, I'd like it to have more testing logs since I'm a big fan of them but that's just my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love the effect, but the tone is really off. Lines like "Its blending mechanism was replaced by a strong jaw that chews up the food." just don't work in a clinical setting. Also, why did the one guy want to self-terminate? That bothers me for some reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The resulting item would probably be no safer than 682. (Nevermind, Varkarrus' note below about 682 is much better.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: (and note that's a thousands separator, not a decimal point. I did a double-take at first, because I'm used to the US method of using a comma for thousands, and a period for decimals)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What I liked most about this was the jaw replacing the blending part of the blender, the organic analogue of a a machine vibe is really effective.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has really got a lot of tone issues. Turned me way off. Downboat toot toot

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
However, the target immediately settled, fatally crushing one staff member, and injuring two others.

What does "settled" mean, in this context?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: D-1415-02 has no sense of adventure

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm with FortuneFavorsBold here. Found several issues that should probably be reworded.
1) Converting that S60 took way more matter than it should have (should have taken 3 kg for a 1.5 ton Volvo).
2) How did the flesh slab "settle" in Test 4?
3) What do you mean by "imprinting" in Test 5?
4) Why does the Volvo not need any nutrition when it's idle? If it's genetically human, it needs as many calories per day per kilogram as a human does.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reduced efficiency, by what the author means for some unknown reason the skip is less efficient when it isn't using human flesh, thus requiring more source material.

think of it as a sculptor who normally carves with soapstone and is given a block of marble to work with, its entirely reasonable to expect the sculptor to take not only longer but require a considerably larger piece of source material they normally work with to make up for any "mistakes"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I like the idea, but there are some tone issues and it is internally inconsistent. "…while destroying tissue in the wearer's body roughly equivalent to 1/500th the mass of the converted object." So how does 1/500th of the mass of a small blender equate to "most of the cow's torso?" Was this a miniature cow?

Also the Foundation forcing a woman to sleep in a gross organic bed? Forcing a researcher to keep wearing her labcoat? Seems a little unnecessarily cruel, rather than cold.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The concept is pretty good, but the tone is really bad. I feel like this one ought to be expanded and explained so that it makes sense without the test log. Give it some manner of story if you can (and are still active to get this message).

-1

item.title: SCP-1415, (Gauntlet that turns things into flesh)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted, though I must say I am a little sad that the experiment log ends after they figure out they can use animals. For multiple reasons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, the armed guards are sorta because despite it being a safe SCP, someone using it could potentially cause some damage. (Sometime after writing this SCP I got the crazy mental image of SCP-682 with it, recovering any flesh lost in the conversion rapidly as it turns a bunch of kitchen appliances into hard-to-destroy omnicidal critters). As for SCP-494, I had never heard of it before but yeah I can see the similarities. Well, we do have 1000+ SCPs on this site…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The experiment logs were vital to selling the SCP. I wasn't too impressed with the setup, but the logs - MAN there was some creepy and gross stuff there, and I mean that in a totally good way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The containment procedures seem a little much. Two armed guards for a Safe?

Other than that, I like this. Any relation to 494? Both are fingerless leather gloves with similar effects. (also hi varky)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
127 has an ordinary casing, this converts the material ENTIRELY.

But yeah, kinda similar. Unintentional of course.

I like this article and I don't know why.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't particularly like this, but now has no one mentioned SCP-127?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thoroughly enjoyed this one, but the line "Termination pending, regardless" is bugging me. First off, regardless of what? Second, why do you need to say anything beyond "Termination pending" in the first place?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wonder if this could be used as a termination method for 682.

Of course you need something that's practically the same size as a METRIC FUCK LOAD. However big that is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Guh, this is so icky. So, so icky. I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As much crticism as this has gotten, I really like the concept. Hahahaha what would happen if someone targeted the "living room" with this thing?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ohmygod you have my approval

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ok, I'll buy it. Also, WHALE MILKSHAKES. I want one now. Not to drink, just to have.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well. This is damn cool. I almost need to make a Tale out of this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry. I'll just… try to stop now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't back seat mod.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Lycan, don't tell people things that are not true. There's no rules about needing approval for public test logs. Authors can open up their stuff for edits at their discretion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You're confusing "sentient" with "sapient".

sen·tient/ˈsenCH(ē)ənt/
Adjective:
Able to perceive or feel things: "sentient life forms".

Oh, and while I'm at it:

Note: D-1415-04 has resisted all instructions to sleep in the bed, due to complaints that it "talked to her, and knew her by name".

Not, you know, because her hand suddenly disappeared and she might be a bit distressed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not just you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You mean a collaboration? Not unless you get Senior Staff approval first. Good luck with that, by the way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It was… THE WORLD'S BIGGEST BLENDER
they use it to make WHALE MILKSHAKES

but no in reality as mentioned it converted cow tissue at a less efficient rate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really liked this, but I do have one question: why was most of the cow's torso used up when it converted a blender? That seems odd considering other tests showed it using, as stated, 1/500th of the target's mass. How big was that blender?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Should I make a public test log for this, by the way?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reminds a lot of 494, but I like this one enough to upvote it as well, and I don't think it was intentional as 494 is a pretty obscure SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, the advice was solid. It's just that a 35x35x35 cm safe is actually 42.875 cm³, not 35 cm³.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 4. PROFIT!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That'd be cool, actually. I could see it now: overuse would lead to massive tumerous growths. Course it's not original and I can't use it now…

The reason why it was left handed was due to the whole left=evil thing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. Build statue of oneself, except about 500 times heavier
2. Slap it with gauntlet
3. Doczilla

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wonder if there is a right-handed version of this out there somewhere. Does it convert matter into replacements for the wearer?

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
oh what

fuck you're right

it was originally 35*35*35 and someone told me to change it to $^3$. More bad advice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know what? I reread this and I was wrong. It does bear a small resemblance to the way 294 works, but not enough to warrant a downvote. I'd like to see the article cleaned up a bit more though, in formatting too. Anyway, changing my vote.

Edit: one thing though, a 35 cm³ wall safe is pretty tiny (something like 5 cm x 3 cm x 2.3 cm). It'd be hard to even fit a door in a safe like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I changed the order, so you have an uninterrupted parade.
I also think I'll add more to test 4 though so it's more creepy, because hey I guess it's just not enough to immediately crush a couple people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The car test is great, as is the bed, but I really didn't like test 5, and I'm iffy on test 3. This still has some potential though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I figured that was the only reason someone would suggest to censor it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the test on turning one human into another (test 3) should be removed, and maybe the stone block one (test 4), since they kind of interrupt the "parade of squicky/creepy things" effect you otherwise have going.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What does name obviousness have to do with the plain fact that the censoring of a name which is a prerequisite for accessing an item is patently stupid?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to second this, most Foundation doctors have incredibly obvious names.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Who the hell told you that?

And yeah, sorry. Bad advice happens everywhere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was uncensored originally (Dr. Tergis, which was Latin for skin), but then I was told to censor it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Heh, probably. I bet it will end up appearing on their author page if they ever get one because in ages past, I done it too.

That said, I don't really mind. What strikes me as correction-worthy is this.

Approval of one (1) Level 3 Personnel, or of Dr. T█████, is required for removal, in which case, Dr. T█████ is to retrieve the object while keeping the password secret.

The name being censored is bullshit, because it prevents you from going through the standard procedure to get access to it. Which you should have clearance for, since you are reading the damn document

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This feels like it could the beginnings of a self-insert character. Otherwise, I enjoyed the article. Enjoy an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Note: Dr.A██ is not to remove the coat, as it appears to have imprinted on her. Further analysis is required.

this is bad. it makes absolutely no sense on any level. a scientific lab wouldn't do that, the researcher wouldn't agree to that, and the foundation certainly wouldn't do that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think you missed the "drop in efficiency" note for use of cow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: People weren't kidding about Wikidot being screwy. Bleh!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me of 604 but it's a gauntlet. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Double post removed.

Wikidot is getting weird. Tried to post. Post failed. Tried again. Post Failed.
Then one of the posts showed up under "Recent Posts" but not this thread.
Later on, both allegedly failed posts appeared on the thread.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No LOLFoundation. You'll get downvoted for that alone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The tone's a little off ("gauntlet", for one), and the test logs have some significant problems with scale. 1/500 of the mass of a blender is probably a few grams, at most; a full-grown Holstein cow weighs around 580 kilograms. Making a blender out of cow would cause just about enough tissue loss to make its skin itch. Also, I fixed a bit of capitalization for you.

Other than that, the idea's pretty okay. Interesting.
